sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: There are three entry points into the B.S. in Economics major. These include students transferring from another institution, students directly admitted into the major from high school, and UWB pre-majors. Regardless of how you enter the B.S. in Economics major, the requirements are the same. The table below outlines the five prerequisite courses and their most common equivalents.

supportLists.listTitle: UW Bothell Bachelor of Economics prerequisites

requirementCueLines: There are three entry points into the B.S. in Economics major. These include students transferring from another institution, students directly admitted into the major from high school, and UWB pre-majors. Regardless of how you enter the B.S. in Economics major, the requirements are the same. The table below outlines the five prerequisite courses and their most common equivalents.
BIS 215 (previously BIS 315), or B BUS 215, or BMATH 21, or STMATH 341
STAT 220, or 221 (221 prior to A’17), or 311, or 390, or
MATH 390, or Q SCI 381, or IND E 315
MATH 112, or 124, or 125, or 126, or 134, or 145, or
